content: Understandably, India has a lever in holding in abeyance the IWT, and it has a moral justification also. But can water security be hyphenated with other strategic concerns? Can it be held hostage to political calculations? Shouldn't it be otherwise that cooperation in securing the right to water and water security be imagined as a low-hanging fruit with the potential of spillover effect in ushering in cooperation in other trickier areas?
